element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Taught through play against progressively skilled opponents
Tutorial inputs shown after every bonus round; player learns through feedback
Strategic positioning; CPU opponent dictates lane switching in single-player until player can respond
Rhythm-based skill challenge; tap button rapidly to succeed
Difficulty escalates through matches toward final boss

element_introduction_order.first_appears: When second player joins during single-player
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dynamic match restructuring; cooperative vs. competitive gameplay shift
Narrative climax; narrative payoff for revenge arc

typical_relief_triggers: Bonus round arm wrestling (breaks combat tension)
typical_tension_triggers: Opponent health depleting but not defeated
Stronger CPU opponent in later tournament rounds
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament bracket structure creates escalating tension through match progression; bonus rounds provide rhythmic relief between battles; best-of-three match format sustains tension across multiple rounds

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Two-lane switching allows optional tactical positioning; players not required to switch rows but gain strategic advantage

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic opponent skill; teaches punch/kick/throw mechanics through simple engagement
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive opponent skill increase; special move mastery becomes necessary; two-lane tactics introduced strategically
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-skill CPU opponents; advanced special move timing required; final boss Geese Howard represents narrative and mechanical climax
notable_spikes: Transition from early practice matches to tournament proper
Geese Howard final encounter (skill and narrative convergence)

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear match sequence where each victory advances player toward final boss; difficulty escalates per round; CPU opponent selection creates narrative flow toward revenge arc climax
notable_level_patterns.example_level: King of Fighters tournament (full single-player mode)

notable_level_patterns.description: Arm wrestling mini-game inserted after every other match; mechanical shift from fighting to button-mashing rhythm; provides relief and skill variety
Many stages feature foreground/background rows; players can switch positions for tactical advantage; CPU controls switching in single-player, forcing player to respond reactively
Second player can join mid-match, converting current battle into two-on-one team fight before playing each other; allows narrative flexibility and cooperative gameplay
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any stage during single-player (multiplayer join point)
